title: Fix CHIRP CSV Import — Persist All 4000+ Repeaters to Canister

requirements.text: Expand the backend canister storage to support bulk insertion of 4,000+ repeaters in a single operation. Add or update a `bulkAddRepeaters` function in the main actor that accepts an array of Repeater records and stores all of them persistently in a stable data structure (e.g., TrieMap or HashMap keyed by a unique ID). Ensure no records are silently dropped or truncated during batch inserts.

quotes: importing over 4000 repeaters and only 17 is showing

acceptanceCriteria: A `bulkAddRepeaters` endpoint accepts an array of Repeater records and stores all of them.
After calling `bulkAddRepeaters` with 4,000+ records, querying the canister returns the full count.
No records are lost between canister upgrades (stable storage used).
The function is callable by authenticated users (Internet Identity principal).

requirements.text: Update the CHIRP CSV parser (`frontend/src/utils/csvParser.ts`) to correctly map all CHIRP CSV columns — including Location, Name, Frequency, Duplex, Offset, Tone, rToneFreq, cToneFreq, DtcsCode, Mode, and Comment — into the backend Repeater type. Ensure CTCSS/PL tone fields are always normalized (e.g., '100.0' → 1000 or the appropriate integer representation the backend expects) and that city/state extraction handles all 50 US states reliably.

quotes: import a CHIRP CSV export file and have all the repeater data saved

acceptanceCriteria: A CHIRP CSV with 4,000+ rows produces 4,000+ parsed Repeater objects with no silent parse failures.
CTCSS/PL tone values are correctly normalized for all rows.
City and state fields are correctly extracted for all 50 US states.
Rows that cannot be parsed (missing required fields) are counted and reported as skipped without crashing the import.

requirements.text: Update the Admin CSV import page (`frontend/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx`) so that after parsing the CHIRP CSV file, all parsed repeaters are sent to the backend `bulkAddRepeaters` canister function in batches (e.g., 200 records per batch) with progress feedback shown to the user. After all batches complete, display the total number of repeaters successfully saved, the number skipped, and any error details.

quotes: allow users to upload the CSV file directly through the browser

acceptanceCriteria: Uploading a CHIRP CSV with 4,000+ repeaters triggers batched canister writes.
A progress indicator (e.g., 'Saving batch 3 of 20...') is shown during the upload.
On completion, a summary shows total saved, total skipped, and any errors.
All 4,000+ repeaters appear in the directory page after a successful import.

requirements.text: Ensure the repeater directory page (`frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx`) correctly loads and displays all stored repeaters from the canister, including those imported via CSV. Fix any pagination, infinite scroll, or query limit issues that could cause only a subset (e.g., 17) of stored repeaters to be displayed. The page should show the full count and allow filtering/searching across all stored repeaters.

quotes: importing over 4000 repeaters and only 17 is showing

acceptanceCriteria: The directory page loads all stored repeaters (not a hard-coded or small limit).
Infinite scroll or pagination loads subsequent pages until all records are accessible.
Search and filter (by state, mode, call sign) work across the full dataset.
The result count displayed matches the actual total stored in the canister.

requirements.text: Require Internet Identity authentication to access the CSV import functionality. Users who are not logged in should see a login prompt instead of the import UI. Authenticated admins should be able to trigger bulk imports, while unauthenticated or non-admin users cannot.

quotes: Internet Identity login required to save and manage repeaters

acceptanceCriteria: Visiting the import/admin page without being logged in shows a login prompt.
After logging in with Internet Identity, the CSV upload UI is accessible.
Non-admin authenticated users cannot trigger bulk imports.
Admin users can upload and import CSV files successfully.

constraints: All repeater data must be stored in the backend canister (Internet Computer stable storage) — no external databases.
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Maintain single-actor Motoko architecture in backend/main.mo.
CHIRP CSV format compatibility must be preserved — do not break existing parse logic, only extend/fix it.
nonGoals: Syncing or exporting data to external services (RepeaterBook, RadioReference, etc.).
Real-time collaboration or multi-user conflict resolution.
Mobile app or native CHIRP software integration.
Editing or deleting individual repeaters imported via CSV (beyond what already exists).
